title: duguetia pedunculata
intro: Duguetia pedunculata, also known as the canelilla tree, is a plant species that belongs to the Annonaceae family. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Duguetia pedunculata are often used to make infusions, tinctures, and decoctions that can be applied topically or consumed orally.
